none.justification: Correctly identifies that plain SET does not type-check, names both SET-specific option-gated paths (GET via setGenericCommand -> getGenericCommand and IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE inline), and points to checkType()/addReplyErrorObject(shared.wrongtypeerr) as the construction site.

ccodegraph.justification: Correctly traces both SET-path call sites to checkType() and identifies src/object.c:887 as the actual reply construction. The stray 'IFGT' in a heading appears to be a typo because the concrete flag list and code are the correct IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE set.

lsp.justification: Correctly names checkType() at src/object.c:884-891 and addReplyErrorObject at line 887, and distinguishes the two SET-specific paths: SET ... GET via setGenericCommand and the compare-and-set options inline at t_string.c:119. It is slightly loose in saying 'SET family' at the end, but the main answer correctly scopes the reachable branches to SET options.
